title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Sergeant Spence and Chief Inspector Grieve meet with Mr. Rolly Claud at the police station to discuss the death of a man in Stagg. The man, who registered the name Enoch Yates, was found with a smashed watch face, a gold lighter engraved with initials, a gold lipstick tube, and heavy steel fire tongs with rust-brown stains.

Rolly explains that he visited the deceased after learning information from Beatrice Ripplecote regarding the status of a man named Robert Andes, who was supposedly dead in Africa but might still be alive. Rolly had first visited his uncle, the lawyer Jeremy Claude, around 8:20 PM. Deciding not to wait for the cautious lawyer, Rolly went to Stagg to confront the man himself.

Rolly arrived at the man's room around 8:30 or 8:40 PM. He attempted to imply the man was involved in blackmail, but the man remained amused. The man claimed he had information to sell and was expecting a "client" that night who would pay for proof that Robert Andes was dead. Rolly, assuming the client was David Hunter, became angry and left the room around 9:00 PM. Rolly expresses regret for his arrogant behavior, noting that the man seemed ecstatic about his upcoming meeting.

When Spence shows Rolly the steel tongs, Rolly notes there was no fire in the room during his visit. Spence reveals that medical evidence shows the victim was struck from behind and then hit from above with the fireplace tool. Rolly expresses a desire for the man to still be alive so he could have obtained the information. When Spence presents the gold lighter and the lipstick tube, Rolly admits he does not recognize them.
